hypothesis: After five rounds of stagnation on the bucketed backbone, minor recipe tweaks are no longer the highest-value action. A fundamentally different but still closely related architecture, `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, may perform better in the operator-mandated `EF=16` regime at `K=32` by avoiding the bucketing path that now appears saturated while preserving the strong two-stage soft-codebook structure.
summary: Run an architecture-only comparison at `K=32, EF=16` by switching from `bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` to `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` while keeping `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed. This follows the severe-stagnation guidance to stop minor tweaks and test a fundamentally different existing architecture on the most valuable exposed small-K frontier slot.
self_review: This is the cleanest way to satisfy the latest severe-stagnation policy without inventing a new family: change architecture only, stay at the highest-value `K=32` point, and keep the rest of the recipe pinned. I chose the non-bucketed two-stage soft-codebook backbone because it is already frontier-relevant at `K=32` under EF=12 and therefore has a stronger prior than more speculative discarded branches.
notes_to_memory: Round 137 exits the bucketed scalar-tuning loop after five non-improving rounds and returns to an architecture-only comparison on the most valuable exposed small-K slot.

`lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` is the strongest non-bucketed prior for this test because it already owns the `K=32, EF=12` frontier point and may behave differently under the now-fixed `EF=16` regime.
Keep optimizer, LR, and aux pinned so the result isolates whether bucketing itself is helping or hurting at `K=32` once the easy recipe tweaks are exhausted.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` improves the `K=32, EF=16` point, map whether the gain extends to `K=64` before returning to scalar tuning.
If it is close but slightly worse at `K=32`, consider one preprocessing or optimizer probe on this non-bucketed backbone rather than reopening bucketed minor tweaks.
If it clearly loses at `K=32`, treat the bucketed family as still the best EF=16 backbone and resume exploration on a more structurally different existing family rather than more LR or aux sweeps.
